zh.lifeSummary: 當Adolf IV. von Berg於1222年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇時，他的父親Hendrik IV van Limburg23歲，母親Irmgard von Berg17歲。他於1240年與Marguerite de Hochstaden結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1259年4月22日在他的家鄉享年37歲，埋葬在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫雷吉隆斯其可 可爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Adolf IV. von Berg于1222年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇时，他的父亲Hendrik IV van Limburg23岁，母亲Irmgard von Berg17岁。他于1240年与Marguerite de Hochstaden结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1259年4月22日在他的家乡享年37岁，埋葬在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫雷吉隆斯其可 可爾。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Berg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lennart, Bjorn, Lars, Nils, Alf, Nels, Thor, Tor, Knute, Selmer, Anders.

German and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill or mountain, from Middle High German berc, Middle Dutch berghe. This surname is also found in some other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. Compare Dutch Vandenberg .
Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from a farmstead named with Old Norse bjarg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

variants.name: Bergh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Johan, Anders, Arndt, Owe, Per, Sigvard, Sven, Syvert.
Scandinavian: variant of   Berg  .
Dutch and English: topographic name denoting someone living on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe, Middle English bergh ‘hill’ (Old English beorg). Compare Dutch Berg and Vandenbergh .

variants.name: Vandenberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Marinus, Gerrit, Gerritt, Hendrik, Jacobus, Maarten, Pieter, Wim, Albertus, Durk, Hendrick.
Dutch (Van den Berg) and Flemish (also Van den Berg): topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berglin
meanings: Swedish: variant of Berg + the suffix -lin, derived from Latin -(l)inius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berget
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Knut.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farm names especially in eastern Norway named with the definite singular form of berg ‘mountain, hill’ (see Berg ).
French: topographic name, a derivative of   Berge   ‘river bank’.

variants.name: Baerg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Kurt.
German (Bärg): variant of Berg .

variants.name: Bergen
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of various places called Bergen.
Dutch and Flemish: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill (see   Berg  ), or a habitational name from any of various places so named (see   Van Bergen  ).
Swedish (mainly Bergén): ornamental or habitational name formed with the (placename) element berg ‘mountain, hill’ + the adjectival suffix -en/-én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berghorst
meanings: Dutch (Gelderland): topographic name for someone living on or by a hill with shrubberies (see   Berg   and   Horst  ).

variants.name: Barg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Mathias, Wilfried.
North German:
topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, Low German barg. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Maren, Johan, Karsten, Alf, Bent, Erik, Gudmund, Knud, Peer, Per, Sten, Thor.
Norwegian and Swedish: variant of   Berg  , from the dative form. As a Norwegian surname, this is a habitational name from any of numerous farms so named all over the country.
German: topographic name for a person by or on a hill, from an extended form of Berg (formerly with a preposition with dative case, e.g. am Berge).

nameMeanings.name: Adolf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Adelwolf, a compound of adal ‘noble’ + wolf ‘wolf’.

nameMeanings.name: IV.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Adolf IV. von Berg was born in 1222, in Holy Roman Empire, his father, Hendrik IV van Limburg, was 23 and his mother, Irmgard von Berg, was 17. He married Marguerite de Hochstaden in 1240. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 22 April 1259, in his hometown, at the age of 37, and was buried in Cologne Region, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adolf IV. von Berg est né en 1222, à Saint-Empire romain, son père, Hendrik IV van Limburg, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Irmgard von Berg, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Marguerite de Hochstaden en 1240. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 22 avril 1259, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 37 ans, et a été enterré à Regierungsbezirk Köln, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adolf IV. von Berg 1222 in Heiliges Römisches Reich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hendrik IV van Limburg 23 und seine Mutter Irmgard von Berg 17. Er hat 1240 Marguerite de Hochstaden geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 22. April 1259 im Alter von 37 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Regierungsbezirk Köln, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adolf IV. von Berg è nato il nel 1222 nel Heiliges Römisches Reich, il suo padre, Hendrik IV van Limburg, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Irmgard von Berg, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Marguerite de Hochstaden nel 1240. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 22 aprile 1259, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 37 anni, e fu sepolto a Regierungsbezirk Köln, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Adolf IV. von Bergが1222年にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチで生まれた時，父Hendrik IV van Limburgは23歳，母Irmgard von Bergは17歳であった。1240年にMarguerite de Hochstadenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1259年4月22日に故郷で37歳で死去し，ドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンレジエルングスベズイルク クoルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Adolf IV. von Berg(이)가 1222년 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hendrik IV van Limburg(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Irmgard von Berg(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1240년 Marguerite de Hochstaden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1259년 4월 22일 그의 고향에서 37세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 레기런그스베지르크 ko에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adolf IV. von Berg nasceu em 1222, em Heiliges Römisches Reich, seu pai, Hendrik IV van Limburg, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Irmgard von Berg, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Marguerite de Hochstaden em 1240. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 22 de abril de 1259, em sua cidade natal, com 37 anos, e foi sepultado em Regierungsbezirk Köln, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adolf IV. von Berg родился в 1222 г. в местности хилигесь рoмищесь раич, его отцу по имени Hendrik IV van Limburg было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Irmgard von Berg, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Marguerite de Hochstaden в 1240 г. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 22 апреля 1259 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 37 лет и был похоронен в местности региирунгсбезирк кoлн, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия.

sm.1246-1259: mai le 1246 e o'o I le 1259

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adolf IV. von Berg nació en 1222, en Heiliges Römisches Reich, su papá, Hendrik IV van Limburg, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Irmgard von Berg, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Marguerite de Hochstaden en 1240. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 22 de abril de 1259, en su pueblo, a la edad de 37 años, y fue sepultado en Regierungsbezirk Köln, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania.
